sourceScope.primarySourceFile: AG 20 - Guideline Concerning Joint Life Functions for 1980 Commissioners' Standard Ordinary Mortality Table.pdf
sourceScope.sourceTitle: AG 20 - Guideline Concerning Joint Life Functions for 1980 Commissioners' Standard Ordinary Mortality Table

observedSectionWindows.note: Title page, acceptability language, and the deduction table introduction.
Blank separator page that should remain visible as a boundary marker.
Ultimate 1xx lookup tables A5-6 and A5-7 with ANB and ALB values.

boundaries: Keep planning focused on this single AG 20 PDF.
Do not widen into AG 21 or later guideline files.
Keep the active guideline posture explicit in every batch artifact.
Keep the page 3 blank separator visible as a boundary marker, not a cue to split the batch wider.
Treat the guideline as review-only table guidance, not learner-facing policy text.
Keep the batch source-bound and review-only.
exclusions: No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No other guideline files in the same wave.

topicMap.label: Joint life functions and equivalent equal-age tables for the 1980 CSO basis
crossReferenceWatchlist: Appendix 5 of the Society of Actuaries Committee on Specifications for Monetary Values
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep this slice within pages 1-7 and do not absorb later guideline files.

proposedBatches.title: Seven-page AG 20 joint-life-functions guideline

pageTarget.notes: Capture the full guideline as a single review-only slice and stop at the page boundary.

proposedBatches.rationale: Capture the full guideline as a single review-only slice and stop at the page boundary.

reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat express duty, permitted treatment, or required table-use language in the joint-life-function context as the requirement; preserve the citation and source context.
reviewStandards.definitionOrTerminology: Treat 1980 CSO mortality table, uniform seniority, Ultimate 1xx Tables, A5-1, A5-6, A5-7, and equivalent equal ages as terminology when the text explains rather than directs.
reviewStandards.calculationStructure: Treat the deduction table and lookup tables as calculation structure until the text clearly instructs a separate duty.
reviewStandards.formulaContext: Treat table references and joint-life-function wording as context until the text clearly instructs a calculation step.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to the Society of Actuaries Committee on Specifications for Monetary Values, Appendix 5, the 1980 CSO table, or the named table labels as mapping notes unless operational text is included.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat the title line and framing sentence as background content unless they impose a duty.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat the one-batch page window as a review-only boundary that should not be merged forward.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat the indexing choice and the blank separator page as requiring human actuarial interpretation unless the text is explicit.
reviewStandards.sourceQualityCaveat: Treat the blank separator page as a review-only caveat and do not widen the batch because the interior page is blank.

appReady: export is sanitized, stable, and versioned
ragReady: review status no longer requires human disposition

currentChecksNeeded: Plan file exists and parses as JSON.
The AG 20 plan markdown exists and carries the expected headings.
Every proposed batch is review-only by default.
The plan keeps the scope to pages 1-7 only.
The source posture is explicitly active.
Each planned batch has a matching batch definition.
The AG 20 batch definition file stays synchronized with the plan.
futureChecksSuggested: One-page / short-guideline checks for future compact guideline files.
No learner-facing, app-ready, or RAG-ready promotion checks.
Source-bound table-context checks once extraction starts.
scriptImplications: scripts/ag20-batch-definitions.mjs should stay synchronized with the planned batch-098 slice when extraction is authorized.
scripts/batch-definitions.mjs should import the AG 20 definition file so the shared runner can find the planned batch ID.
scripts/validate-scaffold.mjs should verify that the AG 20 plan file exists, parses cleanly, and preserves the review-only posture.
The shared runner should continue to honor the same stop conditions used for the VM-20 batches.

notes: Controlled AG 20 planning artifact. Planned batch 098 remains review-only by default and is not an extraction run.
